terms.definition: Loss of phase-coherent order in a quantum or informational system, resulting in effectively classical, higher-entropy behavior.
The lower-bound value that an exponential decay curve approaches but does not cross as generation number tends to infinity.
A process whose rate of decrease is proportional to its current value, producing a curve of the form Ae^{-t/τ}.
Quantitative measure of micro-state uncertainty or disorder in a closed system; increases according to the Second Law of Thermodynamics.
Accumulated misalignment with the Logos that is inherited across successive generations, compounding over time.
The divine rational order or informational template sustaining creation, per Johannine theology.
Hypothesised pervasive background field with a non-zero ground state that maintains baseline coherence in biological and physical systems.
The irreducible vacuum energy present even at absolute zero temperature in quantum field theory.
The degree to which parts of a system maintain consistent phase relationships, enabling information retention and constructive interference.
Theologically posited minimum level of sustaining order and beneficence that God extends to all creation irrespective of moral status.
Non-zero average value of a quantum field in its lowest-energy (vacuum) state, exemplified by the Higgs field’s 246 GeV value.
The time-direction defined by the increase of entropy, giving rise to perceived temporal asymmetry.
Fraction of an original specification accurately transmitted to the next generation; 0 < If ≤ 1.
Statistical measure indicating the proportion of variance in the dependent variable predictable from the independent variable(s).
